data.title: Hong Kong enacts Asia’s first stablecoin regulation with landmark ordinance starting August 2025
data.description:
15-6-2025 – Hong Kong is set to implement its groundbreaking Stablecoin Ordinance on August 1, 2025, as declared by Financial Secretary Paul Chan Mo-po. This visionary legislation heralds a new era for digital asset oversight, positioning the city at the forefront of innovative financial governance in Asia. What the stablecoin ordinance means for issuers and [.
source: https://www.cointrackdaily.com/hong-kong-enacts-asias-first-stablecoin-regulation-with-landmark-ordinance-starting-august-2025/

data.title: Buidlpad: Extending the contribution period by 12 hours, the total amount of donations distributed by the SAHARA community has exceeded 46 million US dollars
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Buidlpad announced on the X platform that based on community feedback, the contribution period will be extended by 12 hours and is expected to end at 3 am UTC on June 16, 2025. The total amount of donations distributed by the SAHARA community has exceeded 46 million US dollars. In addition, related updates include: 
1. Users can now update the bound email, which needs to be verified from the old and new email addresses. 
2. The login cookie is valid for only 1 hour, after which the user will be automatically logged out as a security measure. 
3. Single-session login will be implemented. That is, if you logged in on a computer before and now log in on a mobile phone, the computer login will be logged out. Only one session can be logged in at the same time. 
4. Multiple contributions are now allowed only when the user's first contribution uses the same asset. The restrictions are the same.

data.title: Bitcoin Experiences Slight Decline Below $105,000
data.description:
According to PANews, Bitcoin has recently fallen below the $105,000 mark, with the latest trading price reported at $104,981. 50 per coin. Despite this decline, the cryptocurrency has seen a modest daily increase of 0. 14%.
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/25644860702266?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS

data.title: Glassnode: BTC's upward trend has not changed due to the sharp growth in the previous two cycles
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Glassnode published an article on the X platform stating that although the current market value of Bitcoin has increased significantly compared with the previous two cycles, the development trend of the current cycle has not changed due to the increase in market value, and it is still continuing the growth trend of the previous two cycles.
Data shows that Bitcoin rose by 1076% from 2015 to 2018, 1007% from 2018 to 2022, and 656% from 2022 to date. This trend shows that the growth of market demand keeps pace with the mature development of Bitcoin, and it is also a sign that investors are still optimistic about Bitcoin.

data.title: Vietnam passes digital technology bill to legalize cryptocurrency, expected to take effect on January 1 next year
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The Vietnamese National Assembly approved the Digital Technology Industry Act on June 14, bringing digital assets under regulatory scope. It is reported that the bill will take effect on January 1, 2026, recognizing crypto assets and laying the foundation for broader digital innovation across Vietnam. It is reported that the bill divides digital assets into two categories: virtual assets and crypto assets. Although both rely on encryption or digital technology for verification and transfer, neither includes securities, digital legal tender or other financial instruments. The current task of regulators is to outline the specific business conditions, classification and supervision mechanisms for these asset types. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Ripple and SEC Seek Settlement in Ongoing Legal Battle
data.description:
According to Odaily, Ripple and the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) are aiming to reach a settlement after nearly five years of litigation. Both parties are attempting to persuade Judge Analisa Torres to amend her ruling, lift the injunction against Ripple, and reduce the $125 million fine to $50 million, citing 'special circumstances' as justification for the request.

Cryptocurrency lawyer John Deaton estimates a 70% chance that Judge Torres will approve a victory for Ripple. He suggests that the judge may prefer to issue a final judgment rather than initiate another round of litigation.

data.title: The US SEC and Ripple lawsuit is about to be heard, and the crypto lawyer believes that Ripple has a 70% chance of winning
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News After nearly five years of litigation, Ripple and the US Securities and Exchange Commission hope to reach a settlement, but they still need to convince Judge Analisa Torres to change her judgment and lift the ban on Ripple and reduce its $125 million fine to $50 million, arguing that "special circumstances" justify the request. Cryptocurrency lawyer John Deaton believes that Analisa Torres has a 70% chance of approving Ripple's victory, and the judge may be more inclined to a final judgment rather than another round of litigation. (DL News)

data.title: Joseph Robin Discusses Ethereum Amid Improved U.S. Regulatory Environment
data.description: According to PANews, Joseph Robin, founder of Consensys and co-founder of Ethereum, addressed community concerns about his recent frequent discussions on Ethereum (ETH). Robin explained that his previous reticence was due to advice from former U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission Chairman Gary Gensler, who recommended against public discussions. Additionally, regulatory scrutiny of Ethereum and the decentralized ecosystem contributed to his earlier silence. However, Robin noted that the regulatory environment in the United States has improved, allowing for more open dialogue. He emphasized his commitment to transparency and encouraged the community to express concerns about Ethereum, Consensys, and decentralized protocols to collaboratively address issues.
